unit.subject: English
unit.unitOverview: Celebrate the learner's first year of English through a supported picture-and-word portfolio, six-page mini-book, one-minute presentation and next-goal reflection.
unit.learningPath:
Choose favourite work.
Review 30 useful words.
Listen to the capstone story and project brief.
Make a six-page picture-and-word book.
Practise and share a one-minute presentation.
Complete the checkpoint and supported reflection.
unit.sourceFile: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 Grade 1 capstone

visual.alt: Young learners sharing picture-and-word English portfolios

dictionaryLinks.childMeaning: A round, crunchy fruit that is red or green.
dictionaryLinks.exampleSentence: Musa eats an apple at break.
practiceSentences: An apple grows on a tall tree.
Would you like an apple?
Yes, please, I love apple slices.
I put an apple in my bag.
Apple starts with the letter a.
dictionaryLinks.spellingPractice: Say, tap and trace: a - p - p - l - e
dictionaryLinks.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult, ask the tutor to say apple slowly, use it in one easy sentence and wait for you to repeat.

dictionaryLinks.childMeaning: A flat top on legs where we eat or write.
dictionaryLinks.exampleSentence: My books are on the table.
practiceSentences: The table stands beside the window.
Is your lunch on the table?
Please put the plates on the table!
I write my letters at the table.
We eat together at one big table.
dictionaryLinks.spellingPractice: Say, tap and trace: t - a - b - l - e
dictionaryLinks.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult, ask the tutor to say table slowly, use it in one easy sentence and wait for you to repeat.

dictionaryLinks.childMeaning: The woman who looks after you and loves you at home.
dictionaryLinks.exampleSentence: My mother cooks lovely rice.
practiceSentences: My mother reads with me at bedtime.
Where is Aisha's mother?
Thank you, mother, for my new bag.
I help my mother in the kitchen.
A mother bird feeds her tiny chicks.
dictionaryLinks.spellingPractice: Say, tap and trace: m - o - t - h - e - r
dictionaryLinks.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult, ask the tutor to say mother slowly, use it in one easy sentence and wait for you to repeat.

dictionaryLinks.childMeaning: To hit the ground and jump back up, like a ball.
dictionaryLinks.exampleSentence: I bounce the ball in the park.
practiceSentences: Balls bounce high on hard ground.
Can you bounce the ball twice?
Watch me bounce it very high!
I bounce my ball to my friend.
The ball did not bounce on the grass.
dictionaryLinks.spellingPractice: Say, tap and trace: b - o - u - n - c - e
dictionaryLinks.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult, ask the tutor to say bounce slowly, use it in one easy sentence and wait for you to repeat.

dictionaryLinks.childMeaning: A shape with four straight sides that are all the same.
dictionaryLinks.exampleSentence: The window is a big square.
practiceSentences: A square has four equal sides.
Can you draw a square?
Look, my sandwich is a square!
I made a square with my blocks.
The prayer mat has a square pattern.
dictionaryLinks.spellingPractice: Say, tap and trace: s - q - u - a - r - e
dictionaryLinks.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult, ask the tutor to say square slowly, use it in one easy sentence and wait for you to repeat.

dictionaryLinks.childMeaning: A big farm animal that gives us milk.
dictionaryLinks.exampleSentence: The cow eats grass in the field.
practiceSentences: A cow gives us fresh milk.
Have you seen a baby cow?
Listen, the cow is mooing!
I fed the cow some hay.
The farmer milks his cow at dawn.
dictionaryLinks.spellingPractice: Say, tap and trace: c - o - w
dictionaryLinks.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult, ask the tutor to say cow slowly, use it in one easy sentence and wait for you to repeat.

dictionaryLinks.childMeaning: To look at something and know it with your own eyes.
dictionaryLinks.exampleSentence: I see the moon at night.
practiceSentences: We see stars in the dark sky.
What can you see outside?
Come and see this lovely picture!
I see my friend at the gate.
Owls see very well at night.
dictionaryLinks.spellingPractice: Say, tap and trace: s - e - e
dictionaryLinks.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult, ask the tutor to say see slowly, use it in one easy sentence and wait for you to repeat.

dictionaryLinks.childMeaning: To move along on your feet, step by step.
dictionaryLinks.exampleSentence: We walk to school every morning.
practiceSentences: Children walk safely on the path.
Do you walk or take the bus?
Slow down, let us walk together!
I walk to the mosque with Dad.
We walk quietly past the sleeping baby.
dictionaryLinks.spellingPractice: Say, tap and trace: w - a - l - k
dictionaryLinks.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult, ask the tutor to say walk slowly, use it in one easy sentence and wait for you to repeat.

dictionaryLinks.childMeaning: The clear drink we need every day to stay healthy.
dictionaryLinks.exampleSentence: Please fill my bottle with water.
practiceSentences: Cold water tastes lovely on hot days.
May I have some water?
Here, drink this water slowly!
I water the plants after school.
Ducks swim on the pond water.
dictionaryLinks.spellingPractice: Say, tap and trace: w - a - t - e - r
dictionaryLinks.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult, ask the tutor to say water slowly, use it in one easy sentence and wait for you to repeat.

dictionaryLinks.childMeaning: A place where people sell food, clothes or toys.
dictionaryLinks.exampleSentence: The shop sells warm bread.
practiceSentences: That shop sells toys and books.
What time does the shop open?
Wait outside, the shop is full!
I go to the shop with Samira.
A new shop opened near the park.
dictionaryLinks.spellingPractice: Say, tap and trace: s - h - o - p
dictionaryLinks.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult, ask the tutor to say shop slowly, use it in one easy sentence and wait for you to repeat.

dictionaryLinks.childMeaning: A round toy you can kick, throw or bounce.
dictionaryLinks.exampleSentence: My ball is red and white.
practiceSentences: The ball rolled under the chair.
Can I play with your ball?
Catch the ball, Yusuf!
I kick my ball in the park.
Ball starts with the letter b.
dictionaryLinks.spellingPractice: Say, tap and trace: b - a - l - l
dictionaryLinks.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult, ask the tutor to say ball slowly, use it in one easy sentence and wait for you to repeat.

dictionaryLinks.childMeaning: A seat with a back and four legs for one person.
dictionaryLinks.exampleSentence: I sit on my little chair.
practiceSentences: The chair is beside the table.
Is this chair free?
Please push your chair in!
I keep my bag under my chair.
Grandad rests in a soft chair.
dictionaryLinks.spellingPractice: Say, tap and trace: c - h - a - i - r
dictionaryLinks.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult, ask the tutor to say chair slowly, use it in one easy sentence and wait for you to repeat.

dictionaryLinks.childMeaning: A warm, friendly name that children use for their own mother.
dictionaryLinks.exampleSentence: My mum makes the best soup.
practiceSentences: Mum waits for me at the gate.
Where did Mum put my shoes?
Look, Mum, I finished my work!
I help my mum on Saturdays.
Mum and I walk to the market.
dictionaryLinks.spellingPractice: Say, tap and trace: m - u - m
dictionaryLinks.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult, ask the tutor to say mum slowly, use it in one easy sentence and wait for you to repeat.

dictionaryLinks.childMeaning: To turn over and over while moving along.
dictionaryLinks.exampleSentence: Watch the ball roll down the hill.
practiceSentences: Round balls roll very fast.
Can you roll the ball to me?
Roll it gently, please!
I roll my prayer mat up neatly.
Marbles roll across the smooth floor.
dictionaryLinks.spellingPractice: Say, tap and trace: r - o - l - l
dictionaryLinks.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult, ask the tutor to say roll slowly, use it in one easy sentence and wait for you to repeat.

dictionaryLinks.childMeaning: A perfectly round shape, like a plate or the moon.
dictionaryLinks.exampleSentence: We sit in a circle for story time.
practiceSentences: A circle has no corners at all.
Can you draw a circle?
Come and join our circle!
I made a circle from playdough.
The full moon looks like a circle.
dictionaryLinks.spellingPractice: Say, tap and trace: c - i - r - c - l - e
dictionaryLinks.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult, ask the tutor to say circle slowly, use it in one easy sentence and wait for you to repeat.

dictionaryLinks.childMeaning: A female farm bird that lays the eggs we eat.
dictionaryLinks.exampleSentence: The hen lays an egg each day.
practiceSentences: The hen pecks seeds in the yard.
How many eggs did the hen lay?
Look, the hen has six chicks!
I collect eggs from our hen.
A brown hen sleeps in the coop.
dictionaryLinks.spellingPractice: Say, tap and trace: h - e - n
dictionaryLinks.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult, ask the tutor to say hen slowly, use it in one easy sentence and wait for you to repeat.

dictionaryLinks.childMeaning: To notice a sound around you by using your ears.
dictionaryLinks.exampleSentence: I hear birds in the morning.
practiceSentences: We hear the rain on the roof.
Can you hear the bus coming?
Listen, I hear someone at the door!
I hear my teacher call my name.
Rabbits hear tiny sounds far away.
dictionaryLinks.spellingPractice: Say, tap and trace: h - e - a - r
dictionaryLinks.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult, ask the tutor to say hear slowly, use it in one easy sentence and wait for you to repeat.

dictionaryLinks.childMeaning: A long vehicle that carries many people to town.
dictionaryLinks.exampleSentence: The bus stops outside our school.
practiceSentences: The bus takes us to the market.
Is this the right bus?
Hurry up, the bus is here!
I sit near the window on the bus.
A red bus waits at the corner.
dictionaryLinks.spellingPractice: Say, tap and trace: b - u - s
dictionaryLinks.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult, ask the tutor to say bus slowly, use it in one easy sentence and wait for you to repeat.

dictionaryLinks.childMeaning: Water falling from the clouds in many small drops.
dictionaryLinks.exampleSentence: The rain taps on our roof.
practiceSentences: Heavy rain filled the street with puddles.
Will the rain stop before break?
Bring your coat, the rain is coming!
I splash in puddles after the rain.
Rain gives the thirsty fields a drink.
dictionaryLinks.spellingPractice: Say, tap and trace: r - a - i - n
dictionaryLinks.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult, ask the tutor to say rain slowly, use it in one easy sentence and wait for you to repeat.

dictionaryLinks.childMeaning: An open place where people buy and sell goods.
dictionaryLinks.exampleSentence: We buy fish at the market.
practiceSentences: The market opens early on Saturday.
Can we visit the market today?
Stay close to me in the market!
I carry the market basket for Mum.
Farmers bring vegetables to the market.
dictionaryLinks.spellingPractice: Say, tap and trace: m - a - r - k - e - t
dictionaryLinks.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult, ask the tutor to say market slowly, use it in one easy sentence and wait for you to repeat.

dictionaryLinks.childMeaning: A soft pet with whiskers that purrs and chases mice.
dictionaryLinks.exampleSentence: The cat sleeps on my bed.
practiceSentences: The cat drinks milk from a bowl.
Is that your grey cat?
Look, the cat is climbing the wall!
I stroke my cat very gently.
Cat starts with the letter c.
dictionaryLinks.spellingPractice: Say, tap and trace: c - a - t
dictionaryLinks.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult, ask the tutor to say cat slowly, use it in one easy sentence and wait for you to repeat.

dictionaryLinks.childMeaning: A smooth white board the teacher writes on at school.
dictionaryLinks.exampleSentence: Our teacher writes words on the whiteboard.
practiceSentences: The whiteboard shows today's date.
Can you read the whiteboard?
Come and write on the whiteboard!
I cleaned the whiteboard after class.
A big whiteboard hangs by the door.
dictionaryLinks.spellingPractice: Clap the parts, then copy: whiteboard
dictionaryLinks.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult, ask the tutor to say whiteboard slowly, use it in one easy sentence and wait for you to repeat.

dictionaryLinks.childMeaning: The man who looks after you and loves you at home.
dictionaryLinks.exampleSentence: My father drives me to school.
practiceSentences: My father works in the market.
Is your father home yet?
Thank you, father, for my new book.
I pray beside my father at the mosque.
A father duck swims with his ducklings.
dictionaryLinks.spellingPractice: Say, tap and trace: f - a - t - h - e - r
dictionaryLinks.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult, ask the tutor to say father slowly, use it in one easy sentence and wait for you to repeat.

dictionaryLinks.childMeaning: To send something through the air with your hand.
dictionaryLinks.exampleSentence: I throw the ball to Yusuf.
practiceSentences: We throw the ball in the park.
Can you throw it to me?
Throw the ball gently, please!
I throw my rubbish in the bin.
Never throw stones near the road.
dictionaryLinks.spellingPractice: Say, tap and trace: t - h - r - o - w
dictionaryLinks.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult, ask the tutor to say throw slowly, use it in one easy sentence and wait for you to repeat.

dictionaryLinks.childMeaning: A shape with three straight sides and three corners.
dictionaryLinks.exampleSentence: The roof looks like a triangle.
practiceSentences: A triangle has three pointy corners.
Can you find a triangle here?
Look, my sandwich is a triangle!
I drew a triangle in my book.
The tent makes a big triangle.
dictionaryLinks.spellingPractice: Clap the parts, then copy: triangle
dictionaryLinks.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult, ask the tutor to say triangle slowly, use it in one easy sentence and wait for you to repeat.

dictionaryLinks.childMeaning: A baby bird that has just come out of its egg.
dictionaryLinks.exampleSentence: The tiny chick follows the hen.
practiceSentences: The yellow chick is very small.
How many chicks can you count?
Look, the chick came out of the egg!
I held a soft chick today.
A hungry chick opens its beak wide.
dictionaryLinks.spellingPractice: Say, tap and trace: c - h - i - c - k
dictionaryLinks.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult, ask the tutor to say chick slowly, use it in one easy sentence and wait for you to repeat.

dictionaryLinks.childMeaning: To notice something with your nose, like fresh bread.
dictionaryLinks.exampleSentence: I smell warm bread in the kitchen.
practiceSentences: We smell the flowers in the park.
Can you smell the rain?
Come and smell this lovely soup!
I smell my mum's cooking from outside.
Dogs smell better than people do.
dictionaryLinks.spellingPractice: Say, tap and trace: s - m - e - l - l
dictionaryLinks.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult, ask the tutor to say smell slowly, use it in one easy sentence and wait for you to repeat.

dictionaryLinks.childMeaning: A vehicle with four wheels that a driver steers.
dictionaryLinks.exampleSentence: Our car is parked by the shop.
practiceSentences: The car stops at the red light.
Is that your family car?
Get in the car, we are late!
I wear my seat belt in the car.
A little car turned into our street.
dictionaryLinks.spellingPractice: Say, tap and trace: c - a - r
dictionaryLinks.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult, ask the tutor to say car slowly, use it in one easy sentence and wait for you to repeat.

dictionaryLinks.childMeaning: A very small round bit of liquid, like one tear.
dictionaryLinks.exampleSentence: One drop of rain hit my nose.
practiceSentences: A drop of water shines on the leaf.
Did you see that drop fall?
Careful, a drop of soup spilled!
I wiped one drop from my desk.
Every drop of rain helps the plants.
dictionaryLinks.spellingPractice: Say, tap and trace: d - r - o - p
dictionaryLinks.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult, ask the tutor to say drop slowly, use it in one easy sentence and wait for you to repeat.

dictionaryLinks.childMeaning: The building where you learn to read, write and count.
dictionaryLinks.exampleSentence: My school is close to the park.
practiceSentences: The school bell rings at nine.
Which school do you go to?
Come on, we will be late for school!
I love my school library.
Children from our street walk to school.
dictionaryLinks.spellingPractice: Say, tap and trace: s - c - h - o - o - l
dictionaryLinks.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult, ask the tutor to say school slowly, use it in one easy sentence and wait for you to repeat.

outcomes.learningOutcome: Learner can choose favourite Grade 1 work.
outcomes.evidenceOfLearning: Capstone mini-book, selected portfolio pieces, short presentation and supported reflection.

outcomes.learningOutcome: Learner can use review words in short spoken patterns.
outcomes.evidenceOfLearning: Capstone mini-book, selected portfolio pieces, short presentation and supported reflection.

outcomes.learningOutcome: Learner can listen and respond to a familiar story.
outcomes.evidenceOfLearning: Capstone mini-book, selected portfolio pieces, short presentation and supported reflection.

outcomes.learningOutcome: Learner can make a six-page picture-and-word book.
outcomes.evidenceOfLearning: Capstone mini-book, selected portfolio pieces, short presentation and supported reflection.

outcomes.learningOutcome: Learner can present the book with an adult nearby.
outcomes.evidenceOfLearning: Capstone mini-book, selected portfolio pieces, short presentation and supported reflection.

outcomes.learningOutcome: Learner can reflect and name one next English goal.
outcomes.evidenceOfLearning: Capstone mini-book, selected portfolio pieces, short presentation and supported reflection.

readings.title: Amal's English Year
readings.theme: celebrating Grade 1 words, stories, speaking and early writing
readings.passageScript: Amal opened her learning folder. She found letters, family pictures, games, shapes, farm animals, senses, vehicles, water and town places. She chose her favourite work and made a new book called My First English World. On celebration day she pointed, read a few words and said, 'My name is Amal. I can speak English. I like my book.' Everyone clapped.

readings.title: My Capstone Steps
readings.theme: celebrating Grade 1 words, stories, speaking and early writing
readings.passageScript: Choose three favourite pieces. Make a six-page picture-and-word book. Add labels and short patterns. Practise a one-minute show-and-tell. Share it with your teacher and complete your reflection.

readings.title: Celebration Dialogue
readings.theme: celebrating Grade 1 words, stories, speaking and early writing
readings.passageScript: Teacher: What is your favourite page? Learner: I like my family page. Teacher: What can you say? Learner: This is my mum. I love my family. Teacher: What is your next goal? Learner: I will read more words.

comprehension.question: Where did Amal find her letters and family pictures?
comprehension.explanation: Amal opened her learning folder and found all her work from the year.

comprehension.question: What is the name of Amal's new book?
comprehension.explanation: Amal made a new book called My First English World.

comprehension.question: Say one sentence Amal said on celebration day.
comprehension.correctAnswer: 'My name is Amal.' (or 'I can speak English.' or 'I like my book.')
comprehension.explanation: On celebration day Amal said those three sentences aloud.

comprehension.question: What did everyone do when Amal finished?
comprehension.explanation: The last line says everyone clapped.

comprehension.question: Name two kinds of work Amal found in her folder.
comprehension.correctAnswer: Any two of: letters, family pictures, games, shapes, farm animals, senses, vehicles, water, town places
comprehension.explanation: The story lists everything Amal found inside her learning folder.

comprehension.question: How many favourite pieces must you choose?
comprehension.explanation: The first step says, 'Choose three favourite pieces.'

comprehension.question: How many pages will your picture-and-word book have?
comprehension.explanation: The steps say to make a six-page picture-and-word book.

comprehension.question: How long is your show-and-tell?
comprehension.explanation: The steps say to practise a one-minute show-and-tell.

comprehension.question: What two things do you add to your book pages?
comprehension.explanation: The steps say, 'Add labels and short patterns.'

comprehension.question: Which page does the learner like best?
comprehension.explanation: The learner answers, 'I like my family page.'

comprehension.question: What does the learner say about her mum?
comprehension.correctAnswer: 'This is my mum. I love my family.'
comprehension.explanation: When the teacher asks what she can say, the learner says those two lines.

comprehension.question: What is the learner's next goal?
comprehension.explanation: The learner answers, 'I will read more words.'

grammar.title: Introduce yourself with confidence
grammar.explanation: You have learned so much this year. Say your name clearly and proudly to someone new.
grammar.commonMistake: A quiet mumble is easy to miss. Take a breath first, then say the name slowly and loudly.
grammar.memoryTip: Look up, smile, then speak. That is a strong start every time.
grammar.practice: Introduce yourself to three people: My name is Aisha. My name is Musa. My name is Yusuf.

grammar.title: Show off everything you can do now
grammar.explanation: Look back at your whole year. Name the new things you can do in English.
grammar.commonMistake: Children may repeat the same skill each time. Encourage a different 'I can' in every sentence.
grammar.memoryTip: Count on your fingers and try to reach five different 'I can' sentences.
grammar.practice: Say three new skills: I can read words. I can count to twenty. I can spell my name.

grammar.title: Tell someone your favourites
grammar.explanation: Share what you love most. Say 'I like' and add your favourite thing or activity.
grammar.commonMistake: Some children say 'I am like books.' The word 'am' is not needed here: 'I like books.'
grammar.memoryTip: Put both hands on your heart when you say 'I like'.
grammar.practice: Say three favourites: I like stories. I like drawing. I like Eid.

grammar.title: Name any object with confidence
grammar.explanation: Pick up anything near you and name it. You can name so many things in English now.
grammar.commonMistake: The little word 'a' is still easy to drop. Listen for it just before the naming word.
grammar.memoryTip: Play a quick game and try to name five things in one minute.
grammar.practice: Name three things around you: This is a cup. This is a book. This is a chair.

grammar.title: Add a describing word to any thing
grammar.explanation: After you name a thing, describe it. Choose a colour, a size or a feeling word.
grammar.commonMistake: Children sometimes name the object twice instead of describing it. Ask 'What is it like?' to get a describing word.
grammar.memoryTip: Name it first, describe it second. Two sentences make a strong pair.
grammar.practice: Name and describe three: It is blue. It is small. It is lovely.

grammar.title: Set your next English goal
grammar.explanation: Think about what you would love to learn next, then say it out loud to your teacher.
grammar.commonMistake: Some children describe what they can already do. A goal is something new, not learned yet.
grammar.memoryTip: Point forward as you speak, because a goal is out ahead of you.
grammar.practice: Say three goals: My next goal is to read a book. My next goal is to write a story. My next goal is to spell ten words.

speaking.title: Capstone speaking 1
speaking.instructionsAndModelLines: Practise the opening of your show-and-tell. Say: 'Assalamu alaikum. My name is ___.' 'I am in Grade 1.' 'This is my English book.'
speaking.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult present, listen to my short Grade 1 presentation and give one warm strength and one tiny next step.

speaking.title: Capstone speaking 2
speaking.instructionsAndModelLines: Rehearse your two best skills while a partner listens. Say: 'I can read ten words.' 'I can count to twenty.' 'I can write my name.'
speaking.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult present, listen to my short Grade 1 presentation and give one warm strength and one tiny next step.

speaking.title: Capstone speaking 3
speaking.instructionsAndModelLines: Show a favourite page and say why you chose it. Say: 'I like this page.' 'I like the farm animals.' 'I like reading with my father.'
speaking.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult present, listen to my short Grade 1 presentation and give one warm strength and one tiny next step.

speaking.title: Capstone speaking 4
speaking.instructionsAndModelLines: Hold up three pictures from your folder and name them clearly. Say: 'This is a bus.' 'This is a shop.' 'This is a duck.'
speaking.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult present, listen to my short Grade 1 presentation and give one warm strength and one tiny next step.

speaking.title: Capstone speaking 5
speaking.instructionsAndModelLines: Describe one picture from your folder in two ways. Say: 'It is big.' 'It is blue and round.' 'It is my favourite.'
speaking.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult present, listen to my short Grade 1 presentation and give one warm strength and one tiny next step.

speaking.title: Capstone speaking 6
speaking.instructionsAndModelLines: Present your finished book to the class. Say: 'Thank you for listening.' 'My next goal is to read a whole book.' 'I will keep practising.'
speaking.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult present, listen to my short Grade 1 presentation and give one warm strength and one tiny next step.

writing.title: My First English World page 1
writing.promptAndInstructions: Page 1: draw yourself and write 'My name is ___.' Write your age underneath. This page is the cover of your book.
writing.expectedLength: One picture, 1-3 labels and one short pattern
writing.successCriteria: The picture and words match; I said the idea first; I used the model; I read or repeated it aloud
writing.support: Tracing, copying, word cards, dictation to an adult and oral recording are allowed.
writing.extension: Add a second sentence or question.

writing.title: My First English World page 2
writing.promptAndInstructions: Page 2: draw one thing you learned this year and write 'I can ___.' Add one extra label to your drawing.
writing.expectedLength: One picture, 1-3 labels and one short pattern
writing.successCriteria: The picture and words match; I said the idea first; I used the model; I read or repeated it aloud
writing.support: Tracing, copying, word cards, dictation to an adult and oral recording are allowed.
writing.extension: Add a second sentence or question.

writing.title: My First English World page 3
writing.promptAndInstructions: Page 3: draw your favourite unit and write 'I like ___.' Add two labels, such as farm and duck.
writing.expectedLength: One picture, 1-3 labels and one short pattern
writing.successCriteria: The picture and words match; I said the idea first; I used the model; I read or repeated it aloud
writing.support: Tracing, copying, word cards, dictation to an adult and oral recording are allowed.
writing.extension: Add a second sentence or question.

writing.title: My First English World page 4
writing.promptAndInstructions: Page 4: draw a place you know and write 'This is a ___.' Choose your school, the market or the mosque.
writing.expectedLength: One picture, 1-3 labels and one short pattern
writing.successCriteria: The picture and words match; I said the idea first; I used the model; I read or repeated it aloud
writing.support: Tracing, copying, word cards, dictation to an adult and oral recording are allowed.
writing.extension: Add a second sentence or question.

writing.title: My First English World page 5
writing.promptAndInstructions: Page 5: draw the weather today and write 'It is ___.' Use sunny, rainy or windy, and colour the sky to match.
writing.expectedLength: One picture, 1-3 labels and one short pattern
writing.successCriteria: The picture and words match; I said the idea first; I used the model; I read or repeated it aloud
writing.support: Tracing, copying, word cards, dictation to an adult and oral recording are allowed.
writing.extension: Add a second sentence or question.

writing.title: My First English World page 6
writing.promptAndInstructions: Page 6: draw yourself in Grade 2 and write 'My next goal is ___.' Then read your whole book to your family.
writing.expectedLength: One picture, 1-3 labels and one short pattern
writing.successCriteria: The picture and words match; I said the idea first; I used the model; I read or repeated it aloud
writing.support: Tracing, copying, word cards, dictation to an adult and oral recording are allowed.
writing.extension: Add a second sentence or question.

activities.title: Portfolio treasure hunt
activities.instructionsAndItems: Hunt through your folder for: 1. your neatest writing, 2. a picture you coloured, 3. a word list, 4. work you found hard. Show your favourite to an adult.
activities.answerSummary: Accept complete, relevant Grade 1 evidence with adult support documented.
activities.deliveryMode: Shared UI and live teacher session

activities.title: Thirty-word picture sort
activities.instructionsAndItems: Spread out thirty word cards from this year. Sort them into piles: school, family, animals and town. Read each pile aloud to an adult.
activities.answerSummary: Accept complete, relevant Grade 1 evidence with adult support documented.
activities.deliveryMode: Shared UI and live teacher session

activities.title: Story retell with pictures
activities.instructionsAndItems: Put four story pictures in order. Retell the story using 'First', 'Then', 'Next' and 'Last'. Ask an adult to listen twice.
activities.answerSummary: Accept complete, relevant Grade 1 evidence with adult support documented.
activities.deliveryMode: Shared UI and live teacher session

activities.title: Mini-book making
activities.instructionsAndItems: Make your mini-book. 1. Fold three sheets. 2. Staple the middle. 3. Add your six pages. 4. Draw a cover with your name on it.
activities.answerSummary: Accept complete, relevant Grade 1 evidence with adult support documented.
activities.deliveryMode: Shared UI and live teacher session

activities.title: One-minute rehearsal
activities.instructionsAndItems: Rehearse your talk for one minute while an adult times you. Stand tall, speak slowly, and hold your book so everyone can see it.
activities.answerSummary: Accept complete, relevant Grade 1 evidence with adult support documented.
activities.deliveryMode: Shared UI and live teacher session

activities.title: Celebration showcase
activities.instructionsAndItems: Show your book at the class celebration. 1. Greet everyone. 2. Read two pages. 3. Say your next goal. 4. Thank your family.
activities.answerSummary: Accept complete, relevant Grade 1 evidence with adult support documented.
activities.deliveryMode: Shared UI and live teacher session

assignments.title: My First English World capstone
assignments.instructions: Celebrate your first year of English. 1) Choose three favourite pieces of work and put them in a folder. 2) Make a six-page mini-book with one picture and one word on every page, choosing from letters, family, farm, senses, travel, water and town words. 3) Record a one-minute talk: My name is ___. I can ___. I like ___. This is a ___. It is ___. 4) End by telling us: My next goal is ___.

quizzes.question: Which word completes the sentence: My name ___ Aisha.
quizzes.explanation: My name is one thing, so we use is; are is for more than one and am only goes with I.

quizzes.question: Which word completes the sentence: I ___ hop on one foot.
quizzes.explanation: Can tells what you are able to do; is and are do not go with I hop, and the is not a doing word.

quizzes.question: Which one is a complete sentence?
quizzes.options: cat this | a cat | This is a cat. | is cat
quizzes.explanation: It starts with a capital letter, makes sense and ends with a full stop; the others are just groups of words.

quizzes.question: Which word names a colour?
quizzes.explanation: Blue is a colour; a bus is a vehicle, a farm is a place and hear is a sense word.

quizzes.question: Which word names a person in a family?
quizzes.explanation: Mum is a family member; the other words start with m too but they are a place, the sky and a thing.

quizzes.question: Which word is an action you do in a ball game?
quizzes.explanation: Bounce is an action with a ball; a hospital is a place, water is a liquid and a triangle is a shape.

quizzes.question: Which one is an animal that lives on a farm?
quizzes.explanation: A goat is a farm animal; a bus, a pencil and a river are not animals.

quizzes.question: Which body part helps you hear the call to prayer?
quizzes.explanation: We hear with our ears; eyes see, hands touch and the nose smells.

quizzes.question: Which sentence tells a safe thing to do in the town?
quizzes.options: Stop at the red light. | Run into the road. | Drop litter on the path. | Push people at the bus stop.
quizzes.explanation: Stopping at a red light keeps you safe; the other actions are dangerous or unkind.

quizzes.question: What should you do before you show your capstone project to the class?
quizzes.options: Rub out all the words. | Practise it and check it. | Hide every page. | Skip the reflection.
quizzes.explanation: Practising and checking helps you share your best work; the other choices would spoil it.

liveSessions.title: Launch and choose
liveSessions.beforeSession: Bring the capstone folder, crayons and the current page.
liveSessions.agenda: 5 min hello; 5 min model; 12 min capstone work; 5 min share; 3 min celebrate
liveSessions.afterSession: Show the new or improved capstone part to a caregiver.

teacherNotes.note: Adult support may include reading instructions, scribing a dictated sentence and operating the recorder. Keep the learner's own choices, voice, drawing and language visible.
Treat presentation day as a warm celebration, not a high-pressure performance. Accept live, recorded or one-to-one sharing.
